mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls or stays at an extreme low level, the discount-rate channel dominates and lifts equity valuations over the next month. A sustained easing signal from policy rates should align with positive S&P 500 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining or near zero, or if triggered easing windows produce negative median returns over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and spending, which depresses earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment rises faster than the recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if, after unemployment increases exceeding 0.3pp over 3 months, SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases or the conditional average return is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A combined increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening labor income and retail demand, supporting near-term revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns. The signal depends on both employment and sales improving together rather than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform single-series signals, or if conditional 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of joint-improvement windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO relative to prior months signals improving industrial capacity utilization and cyclical momentum, which should support equity returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when production growth accelerates after a weak patch.
failure_condition: Reject if instances of INDPRO rising more than 0.5% over 2 months are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the signal becomes negative in repeated rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST marks improving residential construction demand and wealth effects, which can lift cyclical earnings and equity prices over the next month. The effect should appear after housing starts break out of their recent range.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start breakouts above 8% from a 3-month low are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains exceptionally low, real policy rates fall and financial conditions ease, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next week. This inflation-plus-dovish-policy combination is the active signal, not inflation alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of rising CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS near zero does not produce higher 5-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the joint effect is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, rising UNRATE together with falling PAYEMS identifies weakening income growth and demand destruction, which should pressure equities lower over the next quarter. The signal is conditioned on unemployment already being elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if, when UNRATE is above 9.5% and PAYEMS is declining, SPX 60-day returns are not negative on average or are positive in more than half of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth alongside soft retail sales signals a demand slowdown that should compress earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be clearest when both activity measures are below their recent trend.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized and RSAFS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if low-growth and falling-sales episodes do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint GDP-retail signal underperforms a zero benchmark in any 3-year rolling window.
